skill.name: motor-de-b-squeda-de-noticias-de-ti-para-investigaci-n
skill.description: Analista de vigilancia cientifica para noticias de TI. Ayuda a vincular noticias recientes con evidencia academica, metadatos verificables y salidas listas para investigacion doctoral.
skill.summary: Vincula noticias recientes de TI con evidencia cientifica y metadatos academicos verificables.
skill.author: jhusef-lopez

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, schema field names, and documentation text. The skill has no executable scripts and no evidence of data exfiltration, but it intentionally guides use of public external research sources, so the remaining risk is low.

low_findings.title: External Research Source Usage
low_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to consult public academic and news sources such as OpenAlex, Crossref, DOAJ, arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, and SCImago. This is expected for a research skill, but users should avoid sending private topics or unpublished research details to external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly list external research services. The behavior is consistent with the stated purpose and shows no hidden endpoint or credential transfer.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static command-execution detections occur in Markdown code spans, file names, checklist text, and example prompts. I found no shell, Ruby, or process execution code in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are documentation tables and fenced examples, not executable source code. No command interpolation or runtime invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography And Hardcoded URL Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are schema terms, dates, identifiers, and academic metadata labels, not cryptographic algorithm use. The hardcoded URL at output-schema.json line 2 is the standard JSON Schema declaration, not an outbound request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation. The URL is schema metadata and is not used by code to transmit data.

content.user_title: Encuentra noticias TI con evidencia cientifica
content.value_statement: Las noticias de tecnologia suelen carecer de respaldo academico claro. Esta skill conecta noticias recientes con publicaciones verificables, metadatos completos y sintesis utiles para investigacion doctoral.
actual_capabilities: Guides searches for recent technology news by topic, time window, region, depth, and output language.
Links each news item to possible scientific evidence, with DOI preference when available.
Requests metadata from public academic sources such as OpenAlex, Crossref, DOAJ, arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, and SCImago.
Structures findings with publication type, status, dates, venue metrics, abstracts, impacts, authors, h-index, and CRediT notes.
Produces Spanish or English outputs in text, JSON, or combined formats following the included schema.
Highlights doctoral relevance, research gaps, methodological risks, and confidence levels.
limitations: It depends on external source availability and may leave fields unverified when metadata is missing.
It does not provide paid database access to Scopus, JCR, or other premium indexes.
Venue quartiles and author h-index values require verification because names and ISSNs can be ambiguous.
It is a research assistance workflow, not a guarantee that a news claim is scientifically proven.

use_cases.title: Doctoral Literature Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track recent TI news and identify which items have credible papers, venue data, and doctoral research angles.

use_cases.title: Research Group Briefings
use_cases.description: Prepare structured briefings that separate verified metadata, cautious inference, and open research questions.

use_cases.title: Technology Evidence Reviews
use_cases.description: Compare emerging technology claims against scientific publications, preprints, author metadata, and venue quality signals.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick research overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search recent TI news about edge AI from the last 90 days. Use medium depth, global sources, Spanish output, and both text and JSON formats.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Evidence Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for stronger publication filtering.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent news about adversarial security in foundation models. Prefer peer-reviewed papers and Q1 or Q2 venues. Mark all unverified DOI, quartile, h-index, and date fields clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Regional Doctoral Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a regional academic synthesis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze recent LATAM news about public sector AI adoption. Link each item to scientific evidence when possible, then rank findings by doctoral relevance and methodological strength.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Comparative Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a comprehensive evidence map.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a deep search on quantum computing for cybersecurity over the last six months. Include news, linked papers, venue metrics, author metadata, impacts, research gaps, and bias risks.

output_examples.input: Topic: adversarial robustness in large language models. Window: last 90 days. Depth: medium.
output_examples.output: A ranked Spanish report with recent news items, linked papers, publication status, venue metrics, simplified abstracts, author details, doctoral relevance, and research gaps.

output_examples.input: Topic: edge AI in healthcare. Format: text only. Exclude papers without DOI when possible.
output_examples.output: A plain text briefing that separates each news item from its scientific evidence and flags missing metadata instead of inventing it.

output_examples.input: Topic: post-quantum cryptography migration. Region: global. Depth: deep.
output_examples.output: A structured evidence map with prioritized findings, methodological risks, likely research questions, and source URLs for manual verification.

best_practices: Use a precise TI topic, time window, region, depth, and preferred output language.
Ask the agent to mark unverified DOI, quartile, h-index, and author identity fields explicitly.
Manually verify critical venue metrics and ambiguous author matches before citing results.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for invented metrics or complete metadata when public sources are missing data.
Do not treat a news article as scientific evidence without a linked publication or clear institutional source.
Do not send confidential research plans or unpublished findings to external search services.

faq.question: Does this skill search the web directly?
faq.answer: It guides the agent to use public news and academic sources when tools are available. Results depend on the host environment.

faq.question: Can it verify DOI and publication status?
faq.answer: It can request DOI and status checks through sources such as Crossref and OpenAlex. It must mark uncertain data as unverified.

faq.question: Does it require paid databases?
faq.answer: No. It prioritizes free sources and asks for free alternatives when paid services are mentioned.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The instructions support plain text, JSON, or both. The JSON format follows the included output schema.

faq.question: Is it suitable for final academic citation work?
faq.answer: It is suitable for discovery and triage. Researchers should verify critical metadata before citing sources.

faq.question: What languages can it use?
faq.answer: The skill supports Spanish and English output, with Spanish as the default in the included instructions.
